command.text: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{h=$1; f=$2; next} $1==h{print f; print $2; next} {h=$1; f=$2}'

raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that lists files with identical content in /workspace. Use find, md5sum, sort, uniq -d? Approach: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | uniq -d -w32? But need paths of duplicates. Use awk to group. Command: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk '...'. Let's produce one line: find /workspace -type f -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{prev=$1; files=$2; next} $1==prev{print files; print $2; next} {prev=$1; files=$2}'. But need all duplicates. Use awk to collect. Simpler: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk 'NR==1{h=$1; f=$2; next} $1==h{print f; print $2; next} {h=$1; f=$2}'. But this prints each duplicate pair. Might be okay. Provide one line.
